steps.title: Focus on mental absorption
steps.content: The claim says Mrs. Sommers becomes engrossed in deciding how to spend a recently obtained sum of money. The supporting quotation must show her mind occupied by that decision. It should not merely show her in a store, reacting emotionally to money, or enjoying a later purchase.

steps.title: Match the claim to the quote
steps.content: The quotation about the question of investment occupying her greatly directly fits the claim. It also says she seemed dreamy but was really absorbed in speculation and calculation, which means she was deeply focused on planning what to do with the money.

steps.title: Eliminate weaker quotations
steps.content:
• Choice B describes how she behaves while waiting to be served, not her deliberation about how to spend the money.
  • Choice C shows a later shopping interaction involving silk hosiery, not the decision process itself.
  • Choice D shows that the money makes her feel important, but it does not show her absorbed in planning how to use it.
The answer is A.
